ddls:
CREATE TABLE "courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course */,
  "course_code" TEXT /* Code of the course (e.g., ARY211, RDE227) */,
  "course_title" TEXT /* Title of the course */,
  "course_type" TEXT /* Type of the course (e.g., Required Courses, Elective Courses) */,
  "theory_hours" INTEGER /* Number of theory hours for the course */,
  "practice_hours" INTEGER /* Number of practice hours for the course */,
  "ects" INTEGER /* Number of ECTS credits for the course */,
  "prerequisites" TEXT /* List of prerequisite course IDs */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instructor teaching the course */,
  "max_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of students allowed in the course */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the course is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the course was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the course */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_courses_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the department was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_head_id FOREIGN KEY ("head_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "course_offerings" (
  "offering_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course offering */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course being offered */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department offering the course */,
  "semester" TEXT /* Semester the course is being offered (e.g., Spring, Fall) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the course is being offered */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instructor teaching the course */,
  "classroom" TEXT /* Location of the course */,
  "schedule" TEXT /* Schedule of the course (days and times) */,
  "max_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of students allowed in this offering */,
  "current_enrollment" INTEGER /* Current number of students enrolled */,
  "is_open" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the course offering is open for enrollment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the offering was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the offering */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offering_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_course_offerings_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES courses ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_course_offerings_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_course_offerings_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Full name of the student */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the student belongs to */,
  "year_of_study" INTEGER /* Current year of study */,
  "gpa" REAL /* Grade Point Average */,
  "admission_year" INTEGER /* Year the student was admitted */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag to indicate if the student is currently active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the student record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the student record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "enrollments" (
  "enrollment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each enrollment */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student enrolling in the course */,
  "offering_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course offering */,
  "enrollment_date" TEXT /* Date the student enrolled in the course */,
  "final_grade" TEXT /* Final grade for the course */,
  "status" TEXT /* Enrollment status (e.g., enrolled, dropped, completed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the enrollment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the enrollment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("enrollment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollments_offering_id FOREIGN KEY ("offering_id") REFERENCES course_offerings ("offering_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollments_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES students ("student_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instructors" (
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instructor */,
  "instructor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the instructor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the instructor */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the instructor belongs to */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the instructor record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the instructor record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instructor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., student, instructor, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., view_courses, edit_courses) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)
